title: decisions docs/decisions ADR
purpose: E3.2 defines decisions docs decisions adr for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness.
scope: Owns `E3.2` only; siblings under `E3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §7 — Branch E — Knowledge & composition plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#7-branch-e-knowledge-composition-plane).
behavior_steps: Before git-workflow merge or program integration, the conductor runs S0 staleness scripts (`update-staleness.py`, reconcile-stale) and reads `docs/manifest/staleness.json` to confirm design artifacts match current pursuit scope.
In program mode, reconcile-artifact-graph marks dependent manifest nodes stale when upstream HLD, DD, or integration manifest changed since last approved design.

git-workflow blocks push and merge when the staleness graph lists unresolved stale nodes upstream of the implement diff—implement against outdated specs is the mistake class in [G5.5](G5.5-mistake-stale-design-staleness-reconcile.md).
Economy workers may not waive merge on stale graph nodes; only the conductor may proceed after operator H2 approval or a completed reconcile-stale pass updates journal `next_action`.
If staleness reconciliation is incomplete or scripts fail, pursuit fails closed at H2 rather than merging—preflight re-runs staleness checks on every wake after dual-write resume.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `E3.2` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `E3.2` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `E3.2`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `E3.2` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_e3_2.py when script exists.
Validate `E3.2` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `E3.2` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `E3.2`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: E3.2 defines decisions docs decisions adr for the agent-driven expert system. Knowledge & composition — catalog, compose-first, staleness. In practice, the system before git-workflow merge or program integration, the conductor runs s0 staleness scripts (`update-staleness.py`, reconcile-stale) and reads `docs/manifest/staleness.json` to confirm design artifacts match current pursuit scope, then in program mode, reconcile-artifact-graph marks dependent manifest nodes stale when upstream hld, dd, or integration manifest changed since last approved design.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-e 2026-06-28 -->

`docs/decisions/` stores ADRs and recorded trade-offs when S3 ambiguity resolves: architecture choices, gate waivers with rationale, program manifest conflicts. Journal Q&A captures session decisions; ADRs persist cross-session authoritative rationale.

S3 turns in Plane B should end in journal entry or ADR ([B1.4](B1.4-s3-architecture-ambiguity.md)). ADRs link from design artifacts and task cards when implement depends on a settled choice. Unlike facts, decisions are repo-authored judgment—not external truth. Staleness may mark downstream design nodes when ADRs supersede prior assumptions ([E5.1](E5.1-staleness-design-graph-staleness-json.md)).
